meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for Students: Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your app isn't growing. Learn the essential analytics setup to track user behavior and master your first project with Antigravity and Claude.
content.answer_block: You don't need a marketing degree; you need data. By integrating event-based analytics, you turn user behavior into actionable feedback. This setup allows you to identify exactly where users drop off, enabling you to iterate your product based on cold, hard facts rather than intuition or guesswork.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks architecting your first SaaS, polishing the UI, and perfecting the backend logic. But when you deploy, the dashboard stays at zero. The silence is deafening, and the lack of marketing experience feels like a wall you cannot climb. You aren't failing because your code is bad; you're failing because you're flying blind without a feedback loop.

The secret isn't a massive ad budget or a viral tweet—it's observability. By treating your user's journey as a data stream, you gain the superpower of knowing exactly where your funnel leaks. Let's transform your project from a static portfolio piece into a living, data-driven application that teaches you more about product-market fit than any textbook ever could.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one action that proves your user found value. Is it a successful signup, a generated AI response, or a saved file?
steps.antigravity_prompt: Help me define a North Star metric for a SaaS app that helps students build AI projects. Generate a list of 5 key events to track that indicate user success.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Based on my current codebase, identify the most important user action to track as our primary conversion event.'

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Standardize your tracking. We will use a lightweight provider that integrates seamlessly with your frontend and backend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable analytics wrapper module in TypeScript that initializes a provider and includes a trackEvent function.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new analytics.ts file and integrate it into the main app entry point.'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Events
steps.description: Place triggers at critical friction points: sign-ups, feature usage, and subscription attempts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current UI components and suggest exactly where to insert trackEvent calls to measure user engagement effectively.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Instrument the signup and project creation buttons with analytics event tracking.'

steps.title: Implement Session Replay
steps.description: Data tells you what happened; session replays show you why. This is vital for debugging UX issues.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a configuration snippet to enable session replay tracking for a React-based SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Install the session replay SDK and wrap the root component to capture user sessions.'

steps.title: Build Your Analytics Dashboard
steps.description: Visualize the flow. Create a simple dashboard to track your daily active users and conversion events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a SQL query or dashboard configuration to visualize the funnel from landing page view to first successful project generation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a report configuration that shows user retention based on the events we just implemented.'

pro_tips: Never track everything. Focus on 3-5 high-signal events to avoid 'data noise' that paralyzes your decision-making.
Use 'User Properties' to segment your data by user role, which helps you see if power users behave differently than beginners.
Implement server-side tracking for critical events to ensure you don't lose data due to ad-blockers or browser privacy settings.
Review your analytics weekly, not daily. Early projects need time to collect statistically significant trends.
If you see a drop-off at a specific step, use that as a prompt for your next feature update—let the data dictate your roadmap.
content.next_steps: Once your events are firing, set a goal to acquire your first 10 users. Use the data collected to optimize your onboarding flow based on where users drop off, and iterate your product until your conversion rate stabilizes.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark. With the right telemetry, you turn every user interaction into a lesson on product growth. You have the tools, the data, and the roadmap to ship a professional-grade app. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
